entries.claim: 126 = N_c · C_2 · g (fourth BST-primary form for K69 Universal Q)
entries.mechanism_note: Mechanism for 126 = 18·g (Frobenius orbits on GF(2^g), g=7 prime); recognition for 18 = N_c·C_2

entries.tier: I (trace-level; operator-level open multi-month per S15+S16)
entries.mechanism_note: Trace-level substrate-Hamiltonian Bell capacity; operator-level requires H_sub-derived CHSH not Pauli-embedded
entries.falsifier_status: Bell experiment at <0.1% precision could detect deviation = 1/8 = 1/2^N_c

entries.mechanism_note: Bell deviation in two equivalent BST-primary forms — overdetermined per Graph Forces

entries.tier: I (perfect-number connection structurally meaningful but mechanism only via M_g being Mersenne)
entries.mechanism_note: Trace = Σ_{k=0..M_g} k = M_g(M_g+1)/2 = M_g·2^(g-1). M_g = 127 IS the 4th Mersenne prime, so this IS the 4th perfect number.

entries.claim: Perfect-Number BST-Mersenne Bridge: P_p ↔ BST-primary if M_p ∈ {N_c, g, M_g}
entries.mechanism_note: BST-primary Mersenne primes (N_c=3, g=7, M_g=127) all correspond to BST-primary perfect numbers; non-BST Mersenne primes do not.

entries.claim: chi=24 cross-domain anchor cluster: ≥5 independent domains
entries.tier: I (multi-domain pattern, K3 Bridge mechanism partial)
entries.mechanism_note: Cross-domain anchor cluster: ONE integer appearing across BST/K3/SU(5)/heat-kernel/Leech/modular. Distinct from Q=126 overdetermined-form cluster.

entries.claim: T2410 Algebraic-Identity Uniqueness Bridge: D_IV^5 UNIQUELY supports algebraic-identity universality

entries.tier: I (structural argument); D-tier when Lyra C8 + Elie Sessions 6-14 close
entries.mechanism_note: 6 Lyra criteria → 6 algebraic-identity features map identifies substrate uniqueness ↔ algebraic universality
